video.title: Tour de France 2018: Stage 2 Recap I NBC Sports

transcript.clean_text: just like yesterday a big crash plays a big part of the finish here in stage two around this corner of big pileup and among the men who go down the one in the yellow jersey yesterday's stage winner fernando gaviria you see him get right back on his bike here but it would be too late to get back in the game because at the finish the world champion peter sagan came oh so close to winning yesterday and wins by just this much today celebrates his ninth career tour de france stage win he has more than just a stage victory he also won green today top sprinter and he will be the man in yellow as the race leader when we begin tomorrow here's what he had to say just after the win peter that was uh one hella a hell of a victory can you take us through those those final moves because it was super tight with colbrelli i'm very really happy for today and i have just say a big thanks to all my teammates because uh they were in the front last 30 kilometers and uh in the end uh i expect something more easy but it was really tough up and down decent turn right and a little bit decent and after starts to climb again and uh yeah i said more uh later to start is better today and the mr he starts with degenkolb and after i just overtake them and i was really lucky with the colbrelli then he doesn't jump me and we we just have news that you also have the yellow jersey so that's a double satisfaction for one day uh that's pretty cool i'm very happy for that yeah i'm very happy 80 years ago the last man who won here in laosho yo was a man with the same jersey as you uh you do it again you're you're you're a special writer oh yeah then uh you know all the stories are is uh repeating in you know all the time over and over again and of course the fans are here to to to to congratulate you uh your fans from your country oh yeah i'm very happy i have a lot of friends especially on this stage my father is here my friends from celina for my uh born like uh home city and uh that is also very nice to thank them and also this nice victory could be also for my son marlon congratulations thanks thanks peter nice that he had family on board to see it welcome to the gmc postgame reporter post race report paul burmeister christian vandeveld and bob roll we're used to seeing peter smiling in a good mood after he won a stage but i haven't seen him that tired and that sweaty and that worn out in a long time very hot day today very little win and incredible speed so all the riders making a huge effort every single mile of the way so peter sagan maybe he made the biggest effort of everybody and he does bring up a very interesting point in that interview patience it was patience he just kept waiting and waiting and waiting and he was thankful to sonny cobrelli who finished second for not jumping him so quickly that gave him a chance to go into full power mode of course he didn't have to sprint against all the spinners because of that crash just under two kilometers to go but still peter said on his instincts and one thing about peter he never falls down no matter how technical it gets that gives him so many more opportunities to win stages again the crash about three kilometers but we go past that to the finish where peter sagan as you guys talked about his team really accelerated very small group of sprinters quite miraculous that arno dimar is there considering his flat tire with about 20 kilometers to go this is what quickstep did yesterday ala philippe's still there but no sprinter to lead out marcus burkhart goes i'm surprised that andre greipel who's there did not do a better sprint but peter sagan waiting patiently in the wings doesn't have the quick step sprinter gaviria to contend with darm damar kicks first and peter sagan is actually behind degenkolf closes the gap to damar and colbrelli finds the wheel of sagan and might have had a chance to win but waited a little bit too long the instincts of sagan and of course his speed and power exactly winning the day yeah i mean cabrelli probably was the most patient but he doesn't have the speed nor the the strength that peter sagan has and let's not forget this was all uphill even though they're still going close to 35 40 miles an hour it was uphill so you have to think about when you're going to launch that sprint 200 meters isn't the place to launch a sprint when you're going just that little bit slower on an uphill drag so patience patience won this race today peter's sitting right there around sixth position there's a better overhead shot you
